verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is primarily ER-localized but exhibits documented partial plasma membrane localization, especially upon calcium store depletion. It is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein with an extracellular N-terminal domain that reaches the outer leaflet via its own transmembrane topology. The N-terminus is extracellularly oriented at the PM, and N-glycosylation facilitates its trafficking and stability there. Although the baseline dominant compartment is ER, the protein reaches the outer PM face through calcium-sensing-triggered state changes and ER-PM junctional translocation, representing a stable but conditional surface presence.
